seoTitle: Add Custom Fields to Contacts in GoHighLevel
metaDescription: Create custom contact fields in GoHighLevel to organize customer data and personalize forms, workflows, and communications.
intro: Beyond the default contact details, GoHighLevel lets you create custom fields to capture the exact information your business needs, keeping your CRM organized and enabling more personalized communication. This tutorial covers creating custom fields, choosing field types, and using them across forms and workflows.

steps.heading: Open contact custom fields
steps.body: Navigate to the section of GoHighLevel where custom fields for contacts are managed.

steps.heading: Create a new custom field
steps.body: Add a new field and choose the right field type for the kind of information you want to collect and store.

steps.heading: Use custom fields in contacts and workflows
steps.body: Apply your custom fields within contact records, forms, surveys, pipelines, and automated workflows.

steps.heading: Personalize communications with custom data
steps.body: Use the information stored in custom fields to personalize emails, SMS messages, and other communications.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for CRM organization
steps.body: Keep your custom fields organized and consistent to support better lead management and segmentation over time.

faq.question: What are custom fields used for in GoHighLevel?
faq.answer: They let you collect and organize additional customer information beyond the default contact details, which can then personalize workflows and communications.

faq.question: Can custom fields be used in forms and surveys?
faq.answer: Yes, the tutorial covers using custom fields in forms, surveys, pipelines, workflows, and campaigns.

faq.question: Do custom fields help with segmentation?
faq.answer: Yes, the tutorial notes that custom fields can improve lead management, segmentation, and business automation.

faq.question: Can I choose different types of custom fields?
faq.answer: Yes, choosing the right field types is one of the steps covered when creating custom fields.

faq.question: Who is this tutorial for?
faq.answer: Agencies, marketers, business owners, sales teams, CRM users, and customer support teams.
